2605.21515.title: Predicting Performance of Symbolic and Prompt Programs with Examples
2605.21515.paper_summary: LLM prompting is widely used for naturally stated tasks, yet it is unreliable it may succeed on a few test cases but fail at deployment time. We study performance prediction: given a program, either symbolic (e.g. Python) or a prompt executed on an LLM, and a few in-domain examples, predict its performance on unseen tasks from the same domain. We use a simple coin-flip model, treating each pass/fail program execution as a Bernoulli random variable, whose success probability is the programs unknown performance. In this model, performance depends entirely on: 1) the observed execution outcomes on test cases, and 2) a prior over performances. We compile empirical performance priors from a corpus of diverse programs and tasks, and find that performance for symbolic programs (e.g., Python) are all or nothing, while prompt programs have a diffuse prior with many nearly-correct programs. This difference explains why a few passing tests can certify symbolic programs but not prompt programs. Building on this insight, we develop RAP (Retrieved Approximate Prior), which retrieves similar tasks and prompt programs from an existing corpus to construct a proxy prior, which is then used to predict performance. We show RAP achieves solid performances.
2605.21515.paper_summary_zh: LLM 提示在自然表述的任務中被廣泛使用，然而它並不可靠，可能在一些測試案例中成功，但在部署時卻失敗。我們研究性能預測：給定一個程序，無論是符號的（例如 Python）還是執行在 LLM 上的提示，以及一些領域內的範例，預測其在同一領域未見任務上的性能。我們使用一個簡單的擲硬幣模型，將每次通過/失敗的程序執行視為一個伯努利隨機變數，其成功概率是程序未知的性能。在這個模型中，性能完全依賴於：1）在測試案例上觀察到的執行結果，以及 2）對性能的先驗。我們從一個多樣化的程序和任務語料庫中編制經驗性能先驗，發現符號程序（例如 Python）的性能要麼全有要麼全無，而提示程序則有著擴散的先驗，包含許多幾乎正確的程序。這一差異解釋了為什麼少數通過的測試可以證明符號程序的有效性，但無法證明提示程序的有效性。基於這一見解，我們開發了 RAP（檢索近似先驗），它從現有的語料庫中檢索相似的任務和提示程序，以構建代理先驗，然後用來預測性能。我們展示了 RAP 能夠達到穩定的性能。
2605.21515.author: Chengqi Zheng et.al.
2605.21515.authors: Chengqi Zheng, Keya Hu, Shuzhi Liu, Tao Wu, Kevin Ellis, Yewen Pu
